bill.short_title: Further Continuing Appropriations and Other Extensions Act, 2024

provisions.rate: at a rate for operations as provided in the applicable appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2023 (as amended by P.L. 118-15)
provisions.duration: through February 2, 2024, except January 19, 2024 for provisions under sections 101(1), 101(4), 101(10), 101(12), 134, and 137
provisions.raw_text: by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting ''February 2, 2024, except as provided in paragraph (4)''
notes: SEC. 101(1) of H.R. 6363 amends P.L. 118-15, section 106(3) to extend the CR end date to February 2, 2024
The original P.L. 118-15 CR date was November 17, 2023

cross_references.description: Extends CR expiration date to February 2, 2024

provisions.description: Creates a staggered CR expiration: January 19, 2024 for appropriations under sections 101(1) (Agriculture), 101(4) (Energy-Water), 101(10) (Transportation-HUD), 101(12) (Military Construction-VA), 134, and 137 of P.L. 118-15.
provisions.raw_text:
by inserting after paragraph (3) of section 106 the following: 

(4) In the case of appropriations and funds made available and authority granted
notes: SEC. 101(2) of H.R. 6363 inserts new paragraph (4) into section 106 of P.L. 118-15
Creates laddered expiration: Jan 19 for certain divisions, Feb 2 for the rest

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Section 106

provisions.description: Modifies the CR baseline for the Rural Water and Waste Disposal Program Account to include direct loans in addition to loan guarantees.

metadata.account_name: Rural Utilities Service—Rural Water and Waste Disposal Program Account

provisions.raw_text: in section 101(1) by inserting after ''except section 729,'' the following: ''and except that the language under the heading ‘Rural Utilities Service—
notes: SEC. 101(3) of H.R. 6363 amends section 101(1) of P.L. 118-15
Inserts 'direct loans and,' before 'loan guarantees' in the first sentence of the second paragraph under this heading in title III
Allows the Rural Water and Waste Disposal Program to make direct loans during the CR period

cross_references.description: Adds direct loan authority for Rural Water and Waste Disposal

provisions.description: Amends section 101(10) of P.L. 118-15 to except section 123 as well as provisions carrying the same restriction in prior Acts.

Section 101(10) covers Transportation-HUD appropriations; this excepts section 123 restrictions

in section 123, by striking ''17 days'' and inserting ''94 days'' and by striking ''17-day'' and inserting ''94-day'';

provisions.description: Allows amounts appropriated in section 124 of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2023 (P.L. 117-180) to be charged for proper expenses under 31 U.S.C. 1553(b)(1), notwithstanding the limitation in 31 U.S.C. 1553(b)(2), through February 2, 2024.
provisions.raw_text: Through the date specified in section 106(3) of this Act, amounts appropriated in section 124 of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2023 (division
notes: Added to P.L. 118-15 by SEC. 101(8) of H.R. 6363

cross_references.description: Overrides limitation on charging expired accounts

provisions.description: Allows apportionment of CR funds for Shipbuilding and Conversion, Navy up to the rate for operations necessary for Columbia Class Submarine (AP), not to exceed $3,338,413,000, notwithstanding sections 102 and 104 of the CR.

notes: Added to P.L. 118-15 by SEC. 101(8) of H.R. 6363

cross_references.description: Overrides pro-rata and apportionment rate limitations

provisions.description: Extends the application of section 747 of division E of P.L. 117-328 (locality pay adjustments) through the CR period, with updated year references. Takes effect on the first applicable pay period beginning on or after January 1, 2024.

Section 747 of division E of P.L. 117-328 relates to locality pay adjustments for federal employees

Effective date delayed to first pay period on or after January 1, 2024

notes: Added to P.L. 118-15 by SEC. 101(8) of H.R. 6363
CR anomaly allowing higher apportionment rate for Secret Service without a specified dollar ceiling

provisions.description: Extends the sunset provision of section 1901(e) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (Countering Weapons of Mass Destruction Office) through 1 day after the CR end date by substituting that date for the original 5-year sunset date.
provisions.raw_text: Section 1901(e) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (6 U.S.C. 591(e)) shall be applied by substituting the date that is 1 day after the date spec

cross_references.target: Section 1901(e) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (6 U.S.C. 591(e))

provisions.description: Allows apportionment of CR funds for Department of Education—Student Aid Administration at a rate necessary to ensure the continuation of student loan servicing activities, including supporting borrowers reentering repayment.
provisions.raw_text: Amounts made available by section 101 for ''Department of Education—Student Aid Administration'' may be apportioned up to the rate for operations n
notes: SEC. 145(a); added to P.L. 118-15 by SEC. 101(8) of H.R. 6363
CR anomaly allowing higher apportionment rate for Student Aid Administration without a specified dollar ceiling
Addresses increased demand from borrowers reentering repayment after pandemic forbearance ended

provisions.description: Modifies the transfer limitation in section 302 of division H of P.L. 117-328 for transfers to Student Aid Administration, substituting '10 percent' for '3 percent' through the CR end date, for purposes of basic operations including student loan servicing, business process operations, digital customer care, common origination and disbursement, cybersecurity activities, and IT systems.
provisions.raw_text: The limitation in section 302 of division H of Public Law 117–328 regarding transfers increasing any appropriation shall be applied to transfers to
notes: SEC. 145(b); added to P.L. 118-15 by SEC. 101(8) of H.R. 6363
Loosens transfer cap from 3% to 10% for transfers INTO Student Aid Administration
Applies only through the date specified in section 106(3) — February 2, 2024
The increased transfer flexibility supports continuation of student loan servicing operations

cross_references.target: P.L. 117-328, Division H, Section 302
cross_references.description: Modifies transfer limitation from 3% to 10% for Student Aid Administration

flagged_issues: SEC. 101(6) cr_substitution modifies section 124 of P.L. 118-15 but the underlying account name is not stated in this bill text — cross-reference to P.L. 118-15 section 124 required to identify the account
SEC. 101 creates a staggered/laddered CR expiration: January 19, 2024 for Agriculture, Energy-Water, Transportation-HUD, MilCon-VA divisions; February 2, 2024 for all others
SEC. 140 allows apportionment up to $3,338,413,000 for Columbia Class Submarine — this is a ceiling, not new budget authority
SEC. 143 and SEC. 145(a) allow higher apportionment rates for Secret Service and Student Aid Administration respectively without specifying dollar ceilings

SEC. 102(a) provides a broad general extension of the entire Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 (Farm Bill) through September 30, 2024, with specific provisions overriding or supplementing in subsequent subsections
SEC. 102(f) rescinds $177,350,000 from biorefinery assistance program unobligated balances as a partial offset
Total new mandatory spending authorized in specific provisions: approximately $77,500,000 (not counted in total_budget_authority per extraction rules)
SEC. 102(d)(9) carves out conservation program mandatory funding (EQIP/CSP/ACEP baseline levels) and supplemental disaster assistance from the general extension
SEC. 102(g) makes most provisions retroactive to September 30, 2023, except the rescission and certain research funding provisions
SEC. 302 reduces Medicaid Improvement Fund by $561,000,000 (from $6,357,117,810 to $5,796,117,810), used as a budgetary offset
SEC. 503 increases Medicare Improvement Fund by $286,795,056 (from $180,000,000 to $466,795,056)
All provisions in this title are mandatory spending extensions or policy riders—no discretionary appropriations; total_budget_authority is $0 for discretionary accounts
SEC. 401 appropriates indefinite sums ('such sums as may be necessary') for child and family services programs through January 19, 2024
SEC. 402 and SEC. 403 extend programs at pro rata portions of FY2023 levels without specifying exact dollar amounts
SEC. 701 is a standard budget enforcement waiver provision exempting Division B spending from PAYGO scorecards and discretionary spending limits
